mapping.a_element: maximum work extracted from multi-heat baths with quantum feedback control
mapping.b_element: maximum work extracted from isothermal feedback controlled systems

mapping.a_element: free-energy difference as a bound on work extraction
mapping.b_element: entropy reduction due to feedback as a bound on work extraction

mapping.a_element: generalized mutual information between system and feedback controller
mapping.b_element: information processing by the controller enabling entropy reduction

mapping.a_element: work required for information processing of the feedback controller
mapping.b_element: thermodynamic cost of operating the feedback controller

brief_justification: Both papers derive the maximum work extractable from a thermodynamic system under feedback control, where the bound is set by a combination of free-energy/entropy reduction and the information processed by the controller. The core machinery is the same: a feedback loop that leverages information to exceed classical thermodynamic limits, with the controller's information processing incurring a thermodynamic cost.
